note: first end-to-end SourceKit-LSP run on the smoke fixture (macOS Xcode 15 toolchain sourcekit-lsp 5.10)

name: field-emission-and-extension-references
notes: Three field-emission heuristics + one navigation-reference heuristic: (1) property_declaration only emits Field when parent_symbol is a type kind or extension_owner is set, mirroring SourceKit-LSP's documentSymbol filter that excludes kind 13 Variable bindings at file scope (e.g. Package.swift's `let package = Package(...)`) and inside function bodies (e.g. `let trimmed = "  Ada  ".sanitized()`); (2) Package.swift's manifest binding falls out of the same rule (no Type parent); (3) typealias is left as SymbolKind::TypeAlias — SourceKit-LSP's documentSymbol response (Swift 5.10) omits file-scope typealias entirely, so the remaining 1 TypeAlias:Endpoints FP is documented kind-mismatch noise rather than a parser bug, and the spec query swift-declarations still expects it; (4) navigation_expression in the function position of a call_expression no longer pushes a Field reference (the ParsedCall already records the invocation), so extension-method call sites like "x".sanitized() resolving to extension String { func sanitized() } no longer over-report. Body hits at the suffix span are preserved so body_search continues to attribute trimmingCharacters to the sanitized method. Also added protocol_function_declaration → SymbolKind::Method so SourceKit's per-protocol abstract methods (Endpoint.encode) line up with Squeezy, taking recall from 0.952 to 1.0.
